assay: 10x Chromium 5′ Immune Profiling + Feature Barcoding v2 (Cell Surface Protein)

oligos.name: Single Cell 5′ v2 Gel Bead Primer
oligos.role: Barcoded gel-bead capture/template-switch primer released in the GEM; adds partial TruSeq Read 1, the 16 nt 10x cell barcode, a 10 nt UMI, and a template-switch (TSO) handle to the 5′ end of cDNA and captures the antibody Feature Barcode.

components.name: Template Switch Oligo (TSO)
components.role: 13 nt template-switch handle (rGrGrG→GGG); also the Feature Barcode capture site

oligos.notes: Doc form 5′-CTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT-N16-N10-TTTCTTATATrGrGrG-3'. rGrGrG folded to GGG.

oligos.name: Poly-dT RT Primer (PN-2000007)
oligos.role: Reverse-transcription primer that anneals to the mRNA poly(A) tail and seeds first-strand cDNA synthesis.

components.name: Poly(dT)
components.role: 30 nt poly(dT) anneals to mRNA poly(A)

components.name: Anchor (VN)
components.role: Anchors primer to the start of the poly(A) tail

oligos.notes: 30 T's shown in the document; VN anchor literal.

oligos.name: Feature Barcode Oligo (TotalSeq-C, Cell Surface Protein)
oligos.role: 5′-amine antibody-conjugated oligonucleotide carrying a 15 nt Feature Barcode identifying the antibody; its 3′ capture sequence hybridizes to the gel-bead TSO handle for direct capture.

components.name: Nextera partial Read 2 (Read 2N)
components.role: Read 2N handle for Cell Surface Protein library amplification

components.name: Capture Sequence
components.role: Complementary to gel-bead TSO (TTTCTTATATGGG); enables capture

oligos.notes: From CG000149 Table 1 (Single Cell 5′ v1/v1.1/v2). Capture Sequence = revcomp of TSO.

oligos.name: Feature cDNA Primer 4 – Forward (PN-2000277)
oligos.role: Forward PCR primer for cDNA + Feature Barcode amplification; anneals to the partial TruSeq Read 1 handle.

oligos.name: Feature cDNA Primer 4 – cDNA Reverse (PN-2000277)
oligos.role: Reverse PCR primer for full-length cDNA amplification; anneals to the SMART PCR handle from the poly-dT RT primer.

oligos.name: Feature cDNA Primer 4 – Feature Reverse (PN-2000277)
oligos.role: Reverse PCR primer specific to captured antibody Feature Barcode DNA; anneals to the Nextera Read 2N handle.

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq Read 1 primer
oligos.role: Read 1 sequencing primer (reads the 10x cell barcode + UMI).

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq Read 2 primer
oligos.role: Read 2 sequencing primer for Gene Expression and V(D)J libraries (reads the cDNA insert).

oligos.name: Nextera Read 2 (Read 2N) primer
oligos.role: Read 2 sequencing primer for the Cell Surface Protein library (reads into the Feature Barcode).

oligos.name: TruSeq Adaptor Oligos (PN-2000094)
oligos.role: Forked/Y TruSeq sequencing adapter ligated onto fragmented cDNA during Gene Expression and V(D)J library construction.

components.name: TruSeq adapter (forward)
components.role: Top strand, ligated to A-tailed insert

oligos.name: Dual Index TT Set A – Forward (P5 / i5, PN-1000215)
oligos.role: Sample Index PCR forward primer for Gene Expression / V(D)J libraries; adds P5 and the i5 index.

oligos.name: Dual Index TT Set A – Reverse (P7 / i7, PN-1000215)
oligos.role: Sample Index PCR reverse primer for Gene Expression / V(D)J libraries; adds P7 and the i7 index.

oligos.name: Dual Index TN Set A – Forward (P5 / i5, PN-1000250)
oligos.role: Sample Index PCR forward primer for the Cell Surface Protein library; adds P5 and the i5 index.

oligos.name: Dual Index TN Set A – Reverse (P7 / i7, PN-1000250)
oligos.role: Sample Index PCR reverse primer for the Cell Surface Protein library; adds P7, the i7 index, and the Nextera Read 2N handle.

oligos.name: V(D)J Amplification Forward (PCR) Primer
oligos.role: Common forward primer for V(D)J (TCR/BCR) enrichment; anneals to the TruSeq Read 1 handle. Paired with target-specific reverse outer/inner constant-region primers.
oligos.notes: Reverse outer/inner primers are gene-specific mixes (Human/Mouse TCR & BCR Mix 1/2 v2) targeting constant regions; not enumerated individually.

oligos.name: Illumina P5 adapter
oligos.role: Flow-cell surface oligo for cluster generation (P5 end).

oligos.name: Illumina P7 adapter
oligos.role: Flow-cell surface oligo for cluster generation (P7 end); appears as its reverse complement at the 3′ end of the top strand.

final_library.source_label: Chromium Single Cell 5′ Cell Surface Protein Dual Index Library (CG000330, Feature Barcode)
annotation_lines: TTTCTTATATGGG = Template Switch Oligo (TSO) / capture handle

evidence.locator: Chromium Single Cell 5′ Cell Surface Protein Dual Index Library (CG000330, Feature Barcode)

reads.primer: Nextera Read 2N (Cell Surface Protein) / TruSeq Read 2 (GEX & V(D)J)

library_generation.title: Cell-surface protein labeling with Feature Barcode antibodies
library_generation.summary: Cells are stained with antibodies conjugated to Feature Barcode oligos (TotalSeq-C) that encode antibody identity and a TSO-complementary capture sequence.
library_generation.note: Cells are Fc-blocked and incubated (30 min, 4°C) with a pool of antibody–oligonucleotide conjugates, then washed to remove unbound conjugate. Each antibody carries a 15 nt Feature Barcode plus a 3′ capture sequence (CCCATATAAGAAA) complementary to the gel-bead template-switch handle.

library_generation.title: GEM generation, mRNA reverse transcription & Feature Barcode capture
library_generation.summary: In each GEM the poly-dT primer reverse-transcribes mRNA and the RT template-switches onto the gel-bead TSO, which simultaneously captures the antibody Feature Barcode — both tagged with the same 10x barcode + UMI.
library_generation.note: MMLV RT extends from the poly(dT) primer, then template-switches at the 5′ cap onto the GGG of the gel-bead TSO (giving 5′ cDNA the barcode/UMI/Read 1 handle). The identical TSO handle base-pairs to the antibody-oligo capture sequence, so the Feature Barcode is copied and 10x-barcoded in the same partition.
library_generation.product:
mRNA path (5' cDNA after template switch):
5'- CTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT[CELL_BARCODE:16][UMI:10]TTTCTTATATGGG[CDNA]GTACTCTGCGTTGATACCACTGCTT -3'
3'- GATGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGA[CELL_BARCODE:16][UMI:10]AAAGAATATACCC[CDNA]CATGAGACGCAACTATGGTGACGAA -5'

Feature Barcode path (capture onto TSO):
|--5'- CTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT[CELL_BARCODE:16][UMI:10]TTTCTTATATGGG ------->
                                                      AAAGAATATACCC[SPACER:9][FEATURE_BARCODE:15][SPACER:10]GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGC -5'

library_generation.title: Post-GEM cleanup & barcoded cDNA / Feature Barcode amplification
library_generation.summary: GEMs are broken, silane-bead purified, and PCR-amplified with Feature cDNA Primers 4, co-amplifying full-length cDNA and antibody-derived Feature Barcode DNA.
library_generation.note: A shared forward primer (partial TruSeq Read 1) plus a cDNA reverse primer (SMART handle) and a Feature reverse primer (Nextera Read 2N) amplify both product classes from the same pooled material.
library_generation.product:
Amplified cDNA (Gene Expression):
5'- CTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT[CELL_BARCODE:16][UMI:10]TTTCTTATATGGG[CDNA]GTACTCTGCGTTGATACCACTGCTT -3'

Amplified Feature Barcode DNA (Cell Surface Protein):
5'- CTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT[CELL_BARCODE:16][UMI:10]TTTCTTATATGGG[SPACER:9][FEATURE_BARCODE:15][SPACER:10]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAG -3'

library_generation.title: Size-based separation of cDNA and Feature Barcode products
library_generation.summary: A 0.6X SPRIselect splits the reaction: large full-length cDNA stays on the beads (GEX/V(D)J) while the short Feature Barcode DNA remains in the supernatant (Cell Surface Protein).
library_generation.note: The bead pellet feeds V(D)J enrichment and 5′ Gene Expression library construction; the supernatant (~short amplicon) feeds Cell Surface Protein library construction.
library_generation.product:
Pellet  -> full-length cDNA -> V(D)J (step 5a) / 5' Gene Expression (step 5b) libraries
Supernatant -> Feature Barcode DNA -> Cell Surface Protein library (step 5c, shown below)

library_generation.title: Cell Surface Protein library construction (Sample Index PCR, Dual Index TN Set A)
library_generation.summary: A single Sample Index PCR on the Feature Barcode DNA adds P5, i5, i7 and the Nextera Read 2N handle to yield the finished dual-index Cell Surface Protein library.
library_generation.note: No fragmentation is needed; the short Feature Barcode amplicon is directly indexed with Dual Index Plate TN Set A (unique i7+i5). GEX/V(D)J libraries instead go through fragmentation, adapter ligation and Dual Index TT Set A.

library_sequencing.note: Reads the 16 nt 10x cell barcode + 10 nt UMI shared by GEX, V(D)J and Cell Surface Protein libraries.

library_sequencing.primer: i7 index read primer (Read 2N / Read 2 handle)
library_sequencing.note: Reads the 10 nt i7 sample index (index read N for the Cell Surface Protein library).
Reads the 10 nt i5 sample index (dual-index, forward-strand workflow).
For the Cell Surface Protein library this reads the 15 nt antibody Feature Barcode; for the GEX / V(D)J libraries the same-position read is TruSeq Read 2 sequencing the cDNA insert instead.

title: 10x Chromium 5′ Immune Profiling + Feature Barcoding v2 (Cell Surface Protein)
description: A droplet single-cell assay (10x Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 5′ v2, Dual Index) that jointly measures the transcriptome, paired V(D)J immune receptors, and cell-surface protein levels from the same cell. Cells are first stained with antibodies conjugated to a Feature Barcode oligonucleotide (BioLegend TotalSeq-C, the commercial embodiment of CITE-seq). Inside each GEM, a gel-bead primer template-switches onto 5′ cDNA and simultaneously captures the antibody Feature Barcode through a capture sequence complementary to the template-switch (TSO) handle, so both molecules share a 16 nt 10x cell barcode and 10 nt UMI. Read 1 reads the cell barcode + UMI, dual i7/i5 reads carry sample indices, and Read 2 reads the cDNA insert (Gene Expression / V(D)J) or the 15 nt Feature Barcode (Cell Surface Protein).

reference.label: Simultaneous epitope and transcriptome measurement in single cells

throughput.summary: Droplet single-cell assay; 10x Chromium 5′ v2 targets 500–10,000 cells per sample. The foundational CITE-seq study profiled 8,005 cord-blood mononuclear cells with a 13-antibody panel.
throughput.cells: 500–10,000 cells/sample (10x); 8,005 cells in the CITE-seq paper
throughput.dna: Antibody-derived Feature Barcode DNA (~180 bp amplicon)

publication.statistical_model: Poisson (droplet cell-capture / doublet modeling); antibody-derived tag counts are compositional and centered-log-ratio (CLR) transformed for normalization.

other.label: Foundational method
other.value: CITE-seq (Stoeckius et al., 2017); commercialized as 10x Feature Barcoding with BioLegend TotalSeq-C

other.label: ADT normalization
other.value: Centered log-ratio (CLR) transformation of antibody-derived tag counts

other.label: Sequencing depth (CSP)
other.value: Minimum ~5,000 read pairs per cell for the Cell Surface Protein library

data_processing.summary: Sample-index demultiplexing splits the pooled Gene Expression, V(D)J and Cell Surface Protein reads; the shared 16 nt cell barcode + 10 nt UMI (Read 1) tie all modalities to one cell. cDNA is aligned and UMI-collapsed into a gene-expression matrix while antibody Feature Barcodes are read directly from the FASTQ, error-corrected by Hamming distance and counted as ADTs, then filtered, normalized (log for RNA, centered-log-ratio for ADT) and graph-clustered.
data_processing.statistical_model: Poisson model for droplet loading and doublet-rate estimation (fixed spike-in fraction). Antibody-derived tag counts are treated as compositional data and centered-log-ratio (CLR) normalized; no negative-binomial model is used.
